title: Combine Two Tables
description: Report firstName, lastName, city, state for every person. Include persons even if no address exists.
constraints: personId is the primary key of Person
addressId is the primary key of Address
Each person may have zero or one address (no guarantee of an address row existing)
key_concept: LEFT JOIN — include all rows from left table even when no matching row in right table. NULL for missing right-side columns.

hints.L1: You need all persons, even those without an address. Which JOIN preserves all rows from Person?
hints.L2: LEFT JOIN Person ON Address.personId = Person.personId. All Person rows kept; Address columns are NULL when no match.
hints.L3: SELECT p.firstName, p.lastName, a.city, a.state FROM Person p LEFT JOIN Address a ON p.personId = a.personId
